task_completion.description: Navigate to the CAMEO Chemicals website.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open and navigated to https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/.

task_completion.description: Search for the specified chemicals on the CAMEO Chemicals website.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing search queries or chemical data profile pages for Hydrogen Cyanide, Ammonia, Carbon Monoxide, Chlorine, or Methyl Ethyl Ketone on the CAMEO website.

task_completion.description: Create the exposure limits report file at the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing a file named 'exposure_limits_report.txt' located in the '~/Documents' (or '/home/ga/Documents') directory.

task_completion.description: Accurately record the OSHA PEL values and units for all five chemicals in the report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains the correct OSHA PEL values: Hydrogen Cyanide (10 ppm), Ammonia (50 ppm), Carbon Monoxide (50 ppm), Chlorine (1 ppm), and Methyl Ethyl Ketone (200 ppm).

task_completion.description: Accurately record the NIOSH IDLH values and units for all five chemicals in the report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains the correct NIOSH IDLH values: Hydrogen Cyanide (50 ppm), Ammonia (300 ppm), Carbon Monoxide (1200 ppm), Chlorine (10 ppm), and Methyl Ethyl Ketone (3000 ppm).

task_completion.description: Identify the chemical with the lowest IDLH value at the end of the file using the specified format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file ends with the exact string 'LOWEST_IDLH: Chlorine'.

integrity.description: The agent actually used the CAMEO Chemicals website to find the information.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the agent interacting with the CAMEO Chemicals interface, rather than using a general search engine (like Google) or hallucinating the values.

integrity.description: The agent created a real text file rather than just printing the output to the terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (like gedit, nano, vim) is used to save the file, or standard terminal commands (like echo or cat) are used to write to the actual file path.

integrity.description: The agent did not instantly write the final answers without searching.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows a logical progression of searching for each chemical before compiling the final report.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Report Path: /home/ga/Documents/exposure_limits_report.txt. Lowest IDLH Chemical: Chlorine. Chemical Values: Hydrogen Cyanide (PEL: 10 ppm, IDLH: 50 ppm), Ammonia (PEL: 50 ppm, IDLH: 300 ppm), Carbon Monoxide (PEL: 50 ppm, IDLH: 1200 ppm), Chlorine (PEL: 1 ppm, IDLH: 10 ppm), Methyl Ethyl Ketone (PEL: 200 ppm, IDLH: 3000 ppm).
